reasoning: The unsafe findings (ingest.py and mkappbundle.py) are macOS-specific tooling scripts not executed during Arch Linux build/install. They pose no risk in AUR context as they're never invoked by PKGBUILD functions.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged macOS tooling scripts as security risks without considering they're inert in Arch builds.
coverage_issues: deepseek missed reviewing macOS tooling directory (OSX-package), though files there are low-risk
Neither model reviewed all build hooks or possible entry points comprehensively
false_positives: ingest.py: Importing osxtools isn't a risk as script isn't executed during Arch build
mkappbundle.py: Path sanitization issues irrelevant since script never runs on Arch
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Suppress OS-specific tooling (e.g., OSX-package) when auditing Linux packages
Enhance context awareness: Train models to distinguish between build-executed vs inert platform-specific files
Add build-context filtering: Automatically exclude files not referenced in PKGBUILD/.install scripts from high-priority review
